result: If I had the power to change the world in one way, I’d make sure that every person—without exception—had consistent access to the essentials that support dignity: nourishing food, clean water, safe shelter, and dependable healthcare. When those basic conditions are met, people are freer to learn, create, and participate fully in their communities. It lays the groundwork for everything else we might hope to build together.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
